privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How theRec collects, uses, and safeguards your information on our screen recording and video collaboration platform.
privacy.controllerBody: theRec.site (operated from Alexandria, Egypt) is the data controller for personal information processed to provide the service. Contact: support@updates.therec.site. Legal business name: [Your registered company name — update before launch].
privacy.s1Body: We collect information needed to provide theRec.site, including: name and email; authentication credentials (hashed passwords; we do not store plain-text passwords); Google account identifier if you use Google sign-in; workspace and team membership data; video metadata and uploaded content; subscription and billing contact details; technical data such as IP address, browser type, device information, and usage analytics; and communications you send to support.
privacy.s2Body: We use personal information to operate screen recording, hosting, sharing, and collaboration; manage accounts and workspaces; process subscriptions; send service emails; provide support; improve performance; prevent fraud and abuse; and meet legal obligations. We do not sell your personal information or video content.
privacy.s2LegalIntro: Where the GDPR applies, we process personal data on the following bases:
privacy.s2Basis1: Contract — to provide the service you signed up for (account, recordings, workspaces, subscriptions).
privacy.s2Basis2: Legitimate interests — to secure the platform, prevent abuse, improve reliability, and support our business (balanced against your rights).
privacy.s2Basis3: Consent — where required, such as non-essential analytics cookies or optional communications.
privacy.s2Basis4: Legal obligation — to comply with tax, accounting, fraud prevention, and lawful requests.
privacy.s3BodyPrefix: We operate under a strict baseline:
privacy.s3BodyStrong: we do not sell your personal files or telemetry.
privacy.s3BodySuffix: We share data only with trusted processors that help us run theRec.site:
privacy.s3Processor2: Cloudflare R2 (or equivalent) for video and file storage
privacy.s4Body: We keep personal data only as long as needed for the purposes described in this policy.
privacy.s4Active: Active accounts: data is retained while your account is open and as needed to provide the service.
privacy.s4Trash: Deleted videos: items in trash may be kept up to 30 days before permanent deletion from our systems and storage.
privacy.s4Deleted: Deleted accounts: we delete or anonymize personal data within a reasonable period, except where retention is required for security, billing disputes, or legal compliance.
privacy.s4Backups: Backups: residual copies in encrypted backups may persist for a limited time before being overwritten.
privacy.s4Logs: Logs: security and operational logs are kept for a limited period (typically up to 12 months unless needed for an investigation).
privacy.s4Billing: Billing records: transaction and invoice data may be retained for several years as required by tax and accounting law.
privacy.s5PaddleTitle: 5. Payments — Paddle as Merchant of Record
privacy.s5PaddleBody: Paid subscriptions are sold by Paddle.com (including Paddle.com Market Limited or an affiliated Paddle entity), which acts as Merchant of Record. Paddle is an independent data controller for payment, billing, tax, fraud prevention, and transaction compliance data. Paddle may collect name, email, billing address, payment method details, and transaction history. theRec.site receives limited subscription status from Paddle and does not store full card numbers. See Paddle's policies at paddle.com/legal.
privacy.s6Intro: theRec.site uses administrative, technical, and organizational measures to protect data, including:
privacy.s6Measure4: Monitoring for unusual or abusive activity
privacy.s6Footnote: No method of transmission or storage is completely secure. We cannot guarantee absolute security.
privacy.s6Breach: If we become aware of a personal data breach likely to affect your rights, we will take appropriate steps, including notification where required by law.
privacy.s7Body: theRec.site is operated from Egypt and uses providers that may process data in Egypt and other countries. Where required, we rely on appropriate safeguards such as standard contractual clauses offered by our providers.
privacy.s8Body: We use cookies and local storage for sign-in, security, language preferences, and analytics. See our Cookie Policy for details. You can manage cookies in your browser; disabling essential cookies may affect core features.
privacy.s9Intro: Depending on your location (including the EU/UK), you may have the right to:
privacy.s9Right3: Delete your data (subject to legal exceptions)
privacy.s9Right5: Restrict or object to certain processing
privacy.s9Right6: Withdraw consent where processing is consent-based
privacy.s9Right7: Lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority
privacy.s9How: Email support@updates.therec.site with subject "Privacy Request". We aim to respond within 30 days. You can export data from account settings where available, or request deletion via support.
privacy.s10BodyPrefix: For access, correction, deletion, export, or other privacy requests, contact us via our
privacy.s11Intro: theRec.site is designed for screen recording, video hosting, sharing, and team collaboration. You are responsible for what you record, upload, or share.
privacy.s11ExamplesLead: Do not upload or share highly sensitive information unless legally authorized. Examples include:
privacy.s11Example2: Financial records or payment card information
privacy.s11Example5: Personal information about third parties without a lawful basis
privacy.s11Action: We may remove or restrict access to content that creates privacy, security, legal, or compliance concerns.
privacy.s12Body: We are not obligated to monitor all content but may review, investigate, restrict, remove, or disable access to content that violates laws, our policies, or platform security requirements.
privacy.s13Body: We may comply with valid legal requests, court orders, and regulatory obligations when legally required, including disclosure of account or content information where permitted by law.
privacy.s14Intro: Paid subscription terms, refund eligibility, and cancellation rules are in our

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.subtitle: Clear policies and fair rules for browser-based screen recording, video hosting, sharing, and collaboration on theRec.site.
terms.introP1: Welcome to theRec.site, an independently operated software-as-a-service (SaaS) platform for browser-based screen recording, video hosting, video sharing, and asynchronous collaboration.
terms.introP2: These Terms & Conditions explain how to use the platform responsibly, outline your rights, and describe how subscriptions and services are managed — so everyone has a clear and fair experience.
terms.introP3: By using the platform or creating an account on theRec.site, you agree to follow these Terms and the related policies, including our Privacy Policy, Copyright Policy, Abuse Reporting Policy, and Refund Policy.
terms.introP4: theRec.site is independently operated and maintained by its founder. The platform is not a marketplace, social network, or public content hosting service. Its primary purpose is private screen recording, controlled video sharing, and team collaboration.
terms.introLegalBody: Service operator: theRec.site. Registered business name: [Your registered company name — update before launch]. Address: Alexandria, Egypt. Contact: support@updates.therec.site.
terms.s1P1: theRec.site may only be used for lawful, legitimate purposes and in compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
terms.s1P2: Account holders are fully responsible for all activity under their account, including:
terms.s1Responsibility5: Content posted or distributed through the platform
terms.s1ProhibitedHeading: You may not use the platform for any of the following:
terms.s1Prohibited1: Publishing or sharing malware or disruptive content
terms.s1Prohibited3: Uploading or sharing illegal or abusive content
terms.s1Prohibited4: Misusing storage resources or service infrastructure
terms.s1Prohibited5: Attempting unauthorized access to the system or user data
terms.s1Footnote: theRec.site may suspend, restrict, or terminate accounts that violate these policies or affect platform stability and security. See our Abuse Reporting Policy for additional detail.
terms.s2P1: All recordings, files, and content you upload or create through theRec.site remain entirely yours.
terms.s2P2: The platform does not claim ownership of your content. Data is used only as needed to run the service and improve the experience, such as:
terms.s2P3: theRec.site does not sell your content, monetize it, or share it with third parties.
terms.s2SensitiveIntro: You should not upload, publish, or share highly sensitive information unless you are legally authorized to do so.
terms.s2Sensitive2: Financial records or payment card information
terms.s2Sensitive5: Personal information belonging to third parties
terms.s2SensitiveAction: If such content creates privacy, security, legal, or compliance concerns, theRec.site may remove or restrict access to the content without prior notice. You remain responsible for the legality of content you upload.
terms.s3P1: The platform provides tools to share recordings and organize collaboration within workspaces.
terms.s3Admin3: Controlling link visibility and content access
terms.s3P2: When you set a recording to "Public" or "Anyone with the link," you understand that it may become accessible to people outside your workspace.
terms.s3Warning: Always review sharing settings before publishing any recording that contains private or sensitive information.
terms.s4P1: theRec.site offers free and paid plans with different storage limits and features. Refund eligibility is governed by our
terms.s4UpgradeBody: When you upgrade to a higher plan, new features take effect immediately. Any price difference may be charged on a prorated basis for the remainder of your current billing cycle.
terms.s4DowngradeP1: When you move to a lower plan, your current benefits continue until the end of the paid period. New plan limits apply automatically at the next renewal.
terms.s4RenewalsBody: Paid subscriptions renew automatically unless you cancel before the next renewal date in your account or Paddle billing portal. Cancellation stops future renewals only; unless a refund is approved under our Refund Policy, access to paid features remains active until the end of the current billing period.
terms.s4MorBody: Paid subscriptions are sold by Paddle.com (including Paddle.com Market Limited or an affiliated Paddle entity), which acts as Merchant of Record. Paddle is the seller of record for your purchase, handles payment processing, invoicing, applicable sales tax/VAT, fraud prevention, and payment compliance. Your contract for payment is with Paddle. theRec.site provides the software service and does not store full payment card numbers.
terms.s4Descriptor: Charges on your bank or card statement may appear as PADDLE.NET* THEREC, PADDLE, or a similar Paddle billing descriptor. If you do not recognize a charge, contact Paddle support or support@updates.therec.site before disputing.
terms.s4CancelHow: You may cancel renewal at any time from your account billing settings or the Paddle customer portal linked from your billing page. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current paid period unless a refund is approved.
terms.s4Chargebacks: If you have a billing issue, contact support@updates.therec.site or Paddle first. Unauthorized chargebacks or payment disputes filed without contacting us may result in suspension of paid access while the dispute is reviewed.
terms.s4ConsumerEu: If you are a consumer in the EU/UK, you may have statutory rights including a withdrawal period for certain online purchases. Where Paddle processes your payment, Paddle's buyer terms and applicable consumer law may apply to the transaction. Nothing in these Terms limits mandatory consumer rights that cannot be waived by contract.
terms.s4PaymentNote: All prices are shown in USD unless stated otherwise at checkout. Paddle may apply local taxes where required.
terms.s5P1: We work to keep the service stable and fast, with ongoing improvements to infrastructure and the platform.
terms.s5P2: However, temporary outages, maintenance, or technical issues beyond our control may occur, including:
terms.s5Footnote: Although we work continuously to minimize downtime, uninterrupted service at all times cannot be guaranteed.
terms.s6P1: theRec.site is not obligated to monitor all uploaded content, but reserves the right to review, investigate, restrict, remove, or disable access to any content that may violate laws, policies, intellectual property rights, privacy rights, or platform security requirements.
terms.s6P2: When necessary to protect users, legal compliance, or platform integrity, content may be removed, public access disabled, or accounts suspended or permanently terminated. No prior notice is required when immediate action is necessary.
terms.s7Body: theRec.site may comply with valid legal requests, court orders, regulatory obligations, and law enforcement requirements when legally required to do so, including preservation or disclosure of information associated with accounts or content where permitted by applicable law.
terms.s8P1: theRec.site services are provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis.
terms.s8P2: The platform is not liable for indirect losses or damages resulting from:
terms.s8Footnote: To the maximum extent permitted by law, our total liability for any claim relating to the service is limited to the amount you paid to us (via Paddle) for the service in the twelve (12) months before the event giving rise to the claim, or one hundred US dollars (USD 100), whichever is greater. This does not limit liability that cannot be excluded under applicable law.
terms.s8ConsumerEu: Nothing in this section limits mandatory consumer rights under EU/UK law.
terms.s9Intro: These Terms work together with our other published policies:
terms.s9Privacy: Privacy Policy — data collection, security, and privacy requests
terms.s9Copyright: Copyright Policy — intellectual property and infringement
terms.s9Abuse: Abuse Reporting Policy — content safety and enforcement
terms.s9Refund: Refund Policy — subscription refunds and cancellations
terms.s9Cookie: Cookie Policy — cookies and similar technologies
terms.s10Body: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Arab Republic of Egypt, without regard to conflict-of-law rules. Courts in Alexandria, Egypt have exclusive jurisdiction over disputes arising from these Terms, except where mandatory consumer protection law in your country requires otherwise. We encourage you to contact support@updates.therec.site before starting formal proceedings.
terms.closingP1: These Terms are not meant to be legal complexity for its own sake. They provide a clear, fair framework for a stable, secure, and easy-to-use experience for everyone.
terms.closing4: Respect for users' privacy and their content

copyright.title: Copyright & Intellectual Property Policy
copyright.subtitle: How theRec.site respects intellectual property rights and handles copyright complaints.
copyright.s1P1: Users may only upload content they own or have legal rights to use, including recordings, files, and other materials shared through theRec.site.
copyright.s1P2: Uploading copyrighted content without authorization from the rights holder is strictly prohibited. You remain fully responsible for the legality of all content you upload, publish, or distribute through the platform.
copyright.s2Body: theRec.site respects intellectual property rights and expects users to do the same. We respond to valid copyright complaints submitted in good faith with sufficient information to identify the reported material.
copyright.s3P1: If the platform receives a legitimate copyright infringement notice, the reported content may be disabled, restricted, or permanently removed without prior notice.
copyright.s3P2: To report alleged infringement, contact us through our Contact page with details identifying the content, your relationship to the rights holder, and a good-faith statement of unauthorized use.
copyright.s4Body: Users who repeatedly upload or share infringing content may have their accounts suspended or permanently terminated, in addition to removal of the reported material.
copyright.s5Body: theRec.site is not obligated to proactively monitor all user uploads, but reserves the right to review, investigate, and take action on content that may violate copyright law or this policy.

abuse.title: Abuse Reporting & Content Safety Policy
abuse.subtitle: How theRec.site investigates abuse reports and protects users, compliance, and platform integrity.
abuse.s1Intro: theRec.site may investigate reports and take action against content or activity involving:
abuse.s1Item6: Sensitive personal information published without authorization
abuse.s2Intro: When necessary to protect users, legal compliance, or platform integrity, theRec.site reserves the right to:
abuse.s2Footnote: No prior notice is required when immediate action is necessary to address serious risk, illegal activity, or security threats.
abuse.s3Body: If you believe content on theRec.site violates this policy or applicable law, contact us through our Contact page with a description of the issue, relevant links or identifiers, and any supporting information. We review reports in good faith and may request additional details.
abuse.s4Body: theRec.site is not obligated to monitor all uploaded content, but reserves the right to review, investigate, restrict, remove, or disable access to any content that may violate laws, policies, intellectual property rights, privacy rights, or platform security requirements.

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.subtitle: Subscription refund eligibility, cancellation rules, and how requests are evaluated on theRec.site.
refund.s1Body: theRec.site offers free and paid subscription plans with different features and usage limits. Purchases processed by Paddle as Merchant of Record may involve Paddle's buyer terms. This Refund Policy explains when refunds may be available and how cancellation affects your access. Contact support@updates.therec.site to request a refund.
refund.s2Body: Monthly subscriptions are generally non-refundable once a billing cycle has started.
refund.s3P1: Annual subscribers may request a refund within seven (7) calendar days from the payment date.
refund.s3P2: Refunds are only available if a substantial portion of the plan's included resources has not been consumed during the refund eligibility period.
refund.s3ThresholdLead: For the purpose of this policy, usage is considered substantial when the customer has consumed twenty-five percent (25%) or more of any primary plan allowance or usage limit, including but not limited to:
refund.s3Allowance1: Number of recordings or videos included in the plan
refund.s3Allowance5: Any other metered or usage-based platform resources
refund.s3Ineligible: If usage reaches or exceeds twenty-five percent (25%) of any primary plan allowance, the refund request may be deemed ineligible.
refund.s3ExampleBody: If a plan includes up to 60 video uploads, uploading 15 or more videos during the refund eligibility period may make the subscription ineligible for a refund.
refund.s3Review: All refund requests are reviewed based on actual platform usage data recorded by the system. To submit a request, contact support via the email on our Contact page within the eligible period.
refund.s4Body: Cancellation stops future renewals only. Unless a refund is approved, access to paid features remains available until the end of the current billing period.

cookie.title: Cookie Policy
cookie.subtitle: How theRec.site uses cookies and similar technologies.
cookie.s1Body: This Cookie Policy explains how theRec.site uses cookies and similar technologies (such as local storage) when you visit or use our website and application.
cookie.s2Item1: Authentication and session cookies to keep you signed in securely
cookie.s2Item2: Security cookies to help protect accounts and prevent abuse
cookie.s2Item3: Preference cookies such as language selection
cookie.s3Item1: Help us understand how features are used so we can improve performance and usability
cookie.s3Item2: Collected in aggregate where possible and not sold to third parties for advertising
cookie.s4Body: You can control cookies through your browser settings. Disabling essential cookies may affect sign-in and core functionality. For questions, contact support@updates.therec.site.
